steps.title: Define the discrepancy
steps.content: Okafor expected herding to appear when peer countries converged on the same currency accumulation rate regardless of their own circumstances. She found that Asian countries showed herding but African countries did not.

steps.title: Explain the regional difference
steps.content: A and B describe reserve composition, not why policy convergence would differ by region. D describes short-term effects of the crisis, not policy-setting freedom. External unions setting individual African policies would prevent peer imitation, while freer Asian countries could herd. The answer is C.
